says DD can you elaborate uh further on
what a refinancing air pocket is and its
key characteristics I'm envisioning it
as being related to monetary policy
movements uh so uh specifically so when
we're talking about global refinancing
air pocket what we're looking at is a
condition whereby the lagged growth of
total nonfinancial sector debt globally
is accelerating and you have a
deceleration in the growth rate of
global liquidity which ultimately means
that the balance Global balance sheet is
not expanding whether it be you know
public sector balance sheet or private
sector balance sheet the aggregate
Global balance sheet is not expanding
fast enough to account for the uh uh
refinancing needs uh that have developed
in asset markets as a function of you
know yester Year's prior debt growth and
so uh we have a significant acceleration
in um you know total fin non-financial
sector debt growth on the precibus as we
progress throughout 2025 and you know
into the latter parts of 2025 and so
that necessitates that we have a
significant acceleration in global
liquidity to offset that uh and we may
or may not have a significant
acceleration in global liquidity
obviously our thoughts on that are
reserve for our uh you know clients here
42 macros so um the key takeaway is that
you know every time we've had a
significant draw down in the S&P and
broader risk assets you know since the
start of the global financial crisis
it's come as a function of a global
refinancing air pocket where we've had a
pullback uh in uh the um in in
refinancing and overall financing
conditions uh globally and that usually
is coincided with a reduction in global
liquidity or in some cases acceleration
of global liquidity that did not keep
Pace with the amount of balance sheet
space required to meet those
refinancings and all the additional new
uh net financing that occurs you know
from the both the PIV private and public
sector in any given year so uh that's
something we're concerned about as a
potential negative Catalyst

offsetting that could potentially

ultimately means is that investors need
